text:
There was a garden filled with red flowers that bloomed at dusk. In each bloom lived a tiny creature—a butterfly, hummingbird, or bumblebee—who emerged in the fading light to find their way to the stars. On this particular summer's eve, a particularly shy hummingbird named Hugo was about to embark on his nightly voyage when he saw something he wasn't supposed to see.

In the middle of the garden stood an old willow tree, its leaves dancing in the gentle wind. Hugo's gaze drifted upward, and he realized he wasn't alone. A translucent figure hovered not far above, a shimmering spirit of the night. The spirit's eyes were as gentle as the moonlight but carried an ancient wisdom.

The spirit beckoned, speaking in words as soft as the rustling leaves, "Hugo, you are a seeker of light, and tonight, you carry something incredibly precious within you—a spark of the stars. Fear not the shadows; they are part of your journey
